traceability.overview: Full traceability mapping for EPIC-1 covering 3 stories (S-1.1 Task CRUD, S-1.2 Real-Time Sync, S-1.3 Advanced Filtering). 24 acceptance criteria mapped to 42 tests across unit, integration, component, and E2E levels. Overall coverage is 95.8% with all P0 criteria at 100% coverage.

detailedMapping.criterion: Users can create a new task with title, description, priority, due date, and tags

tests.testName: Create task with all required fields
tests.given: An authenticated user on the task creation form
tests.when: The user fills in title, description, priority, due date, and tags then submits
tests.then: A new task is created and appears in the task list with all fields correctly saved
and: The task has a unique generated ID
The createdAt timestamp is set to current time

tests.testName: Create task with minimum required fields
tests.given: A valid task creation request with only title
tests.when: The create task API is called
tests.then: The task is created with defaults for optional fields

detailedMapping.coverageNotes: Both happy-path and minimum-fields scenarios covered at unit and integration levels.

detailedMapping.criterion: Users can update any field of an existing task, with changes persisted immediately

tests.given: An existing task in the database
tests.when: The user updates the title and description
tests.then: The task is updated and the updatedAt timestamp changes

tests.given: A task list with multiple tasks sorted by priority
tests.when: A task priority is changed from low to critical
tests.then: The task list re-sorts and the updated task appears in its new position

detailedMapping.coverageNotes: Update operations tested at integration and E2E levels including sort-side-effects.

detailedMapping.criterion: Task changes sync across all connected clients within 500ms via WebSocket

tests.testName: WebSocket sync delivers task update within 500ms
tests.given: Two connected WebSocket clients subscribed to the same project
tests.then: Client B receives the update within 500ms
and: The update payload contains the complete task object

detailedMapping.coverageNotes: Timing assertion verifies sub-500ms delivery with mock WebSocket server.

gapAnalysis.summary: One P2 gap identified: offline conflict resolution edge cases for simultaneous edits. All P0 and P1 criteria are fully covered. No critical gaps remain.

medium.gap: Offline conflict resolution when two users edit the same task field simultaneously while both offline
medium.impact: Users could lose edits in rare offline-collision scenarios, though the last-write-wins strategy handles most cases
medium.recommendation: Add integration tests simulating dual-offline edit scenarios with conflict detection

qualityAssessment.summary: Test quality is good with strong coverage across all priority levels. BDD format is consistently applied. Minor improvement opportunities in edge-case coverage for offline scenarios.

findings.finding: All P0 tests use proper Given/When/Then format with clear assertions
findings.recommendation: Continue using this pattern for new tests

findings.finding: Some integration tests share database state between test cases
findings.recommendation: Implement per-test database seeding to improve isolation

recommendations.recommendation: Add contract tests between TaskService and WebSocket layer to catch integration drift early
Implement visual regression tests for the task card component to catch UI regressions

evidenceSummary.nfrStatus: All NFR targets met: P95 response time 180ms (target <200ms), WebSocket sync P99 420ms (target <500ms)
evidenceSummary.regressionStatus: No regressions detected in 5 consecutive CI runs
evidenceSummary.performanceStatus: All performance benchmarks passing with 10% headroom

p0.notes: All 8 P0 criteria fully covered and passing

p2.notes: 1 P2 gap for offline conflict resolution

gateDecision.rationale: EPIC-1 passes all P0/P1 gates and performance targets. One P2 gap remains for offline conflict resolution that needs a remediation plan before the next release. Approving with conditions to address the gap in Sprint 12.

residualRisks.risk: Offline dual-edit conflict could cause data loss in rare scenarios where two users edit the same field while offline
residualRisks.mitigation: Last-write-wins strategy covers 95% of cases; full conflict resolution planned for Sprint 12

recommendations.recommendation: Prioritize offline conflict resolution tests in Sprint 12 to close the P2 gap
Add synthetic monitoring for WebSocket sync latency in production

nextSteps.step: Create tickets for offline conflict resolution test scenarios
Set up production WebSocket latency monitoring dashboard
Schedule follow-up traceability review after Sprint 12

signOff.comments: Approving with the condition that the P2 offline conflict gap is addressed in Sprint 12. All critical and high-priority criteria are well covered.
